sectionOrderRules: Hero must state what the company manufactures or refines and include a primary contact CTA.
Product summary must describe concrete product categories or supply areas, not vague industrial wording.
Trust proof must appear before the final contact CTA.
Applications summary may appear before trust proof when it helps clarify relevance for target customers.
Sections must avoid repeating the same claims about quality, safety or reliability.
Products intro must connect the business identity to its actual product areas.
Product list items must be concrete and grouped in a way that fits industrial buyers, for example fuels, oils, bitumen or related refined products.
If pricing-overview is used, it should describe quotation-based pricing or commercial setup honestly rather than fixed consumer prices when pricing is not public.
Applications intro must explain which industries, operations or use cases the company serves.
Application list must focus on practical fit and customer needs rather than generic marketing language.
Trust proof, if shown, should support relevance without duplicating the entire about page.
About story must be specific about the company, its operations or market focus and avoid empty claims.
Team should only be shown when real people or functions are available in the dossier.
Facility summary may be included when it clarifies production, storage, logistics or service capability.
Contact info must include real phone, email and address details when available.
Map and opening-hours should support contact and visits, not replace the main contact details.
Contact CTA should make clear what kinds of enquiries are welcome, such as product questions, deliveries or business dialogue.
